item.title: Potential Error
item.content:encoded:
"Personnel must not remain in SCP-2438 for more 6 consecutive hours at any time."

Did you mean to say more than?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This took an unexpected HARD left turn that took me completely by surprise and I love it! Upvoted with a smile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think they tagged the article based on the original anomaly, not the rabbits. I was debating asking them about that, but I forgot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this be tagged as "memory-affecting"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then someone needs to tell the Florida school board, because they taught us about r/k species in AP bio this year

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<.< ….. >.> ….

I put some info in The Leak...

Edit: If you have any questions/comments, it's probably be good to respond here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the loosest modern sense of "rabbits you can't see", yeah. One of the sighting logs actually calls them harvies, so the joke's already been made. Might also be some connection given the location and nature of the menhir.

Side note @Petrograd: might change the note about "animal-fat based biofuel" to "animal-biomass based biofuel". You can actually starve to death eating only rabbit because they're mostly muscle with little fat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So….. pookas?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ARD is correct. I have some thoughts on their reproduction and the forces behind their modification, but I kinda want to be coy about them for now.

I ended up doing the "Not accepted" thing because I wanted to imply that Madeline Weaver is actively watching who's accessing the document for people who might get what's going on.

Edit: Also I posted a name in Series 3. It might be a little much, I'm not sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, it doesn't exactly look like it's working…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They aren't invisible. If you spook the parasitized rabbits (which, being rabbits, happens if you look at them funny), they "emit" an airborne amnestic compound. Essentially, if you spook the parasitized rabbits they fart strong amnestics. The Foundation tried to use the rabbits as amnestics factories, but they screwed up the containment protocols somewhere along the way and let some rabbits escape. Those escaped rabbits made their way onto nearby oil tankers and airplanes and now they've gone global.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get this. c.c I may have started zoning out toward the end, but why are the rabbits suddenly invisible? Is this what the parasites are moving toward? I was surprised nothing was mentioned about them reproducing. I also thought the "credentials not accepted" was unnecessary and kind of goofy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since it has come up a couple times in this thread, r/k-selection theory was disproved empirically in the 90s. It isn't valid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "What rabbits? I don't see any rabbits over hare"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this even containable? We'd need mass distribution of mnestics to the general public to even stand a chance of controlling these things. How long before they start slowly consuming all the plant biomass in the world?

Great article, +1. I didn't like the "call up the MTF at any time for Nesquick disposal" part, though. Direct extermination is the least effective way to control r-selection pests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My goodness, that was a fun article. The concept is pretty neat, and watching the anomaly escalate from anomalous parasites, to antimemtic rabbit infestations was particularly interesting. I also found the notes at the end, in which they are going over all locations where they found the rabbits is particularly enjoyable.

+1. Good show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Antimemetic Rabbits stemming from Protozoan Parasites…+1 good sir

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a lot of fun - I'm a sucker for skips that swerve hard in mid-piece, without losing their underlying theme. And I think that the changes you've made since the draft have sharpened this up considerably. But mostly I love the idea of a protozoan Celtic hearth god who just found an *amazing* bunch of new disciples!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Been waiting for a good antimemetic skip for a while now, and this one does it quite nicely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny at first, fridge horror later: rabbits are the closest thing mammalians have to r-type breeders, and the only things keeping their population down are starvation and predation.
Antimemetic bunnies that don't need to eat? Man, that would be the mother of all invasive species.
…Okay, still kind of funny. Especially when you imagine the handful of people in the Foundation taking mnestics, and seeing a sea of fluffy little wabbits from coast to coast. 'Where the fuck did all these rabbits come from!?'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredible. Start with a biological anomaly, end with the world crawling with secret bunnies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I laughed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Menhir ( I'll see if I can clarify that when in awake for real.)

Edit: Oh, crap, I see what you meant. Thanks, got it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept but I think there's a few typos here and there. Did you mean stone marker?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anti-memetic rabbits.

Ahahahaha the Foundation is fucked, isn't it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Be vewy vewy quiet. I'm hunting…

…shit, what was I hunting again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now is the time where I get sick of staring at this in my sandbox and give it a go.

Original image "Knocknakilla_Standing_Stone" is by Ceoil, licensed under CC BY-SA 3.0, modified by me., Original image 2 Blason ville fr Corbenay (Haute-Saône).svg is by Tretinville, licensed under CC BY-SA 3.0, modified by me.

Thanks to Hercules Rockefeller, sirpudding, AidenEldritch (all of whom put up with me for multiple rounds) as well as pizzaplate, InitHello, and psul for feedback! Thanks to Metaphysician for the background/image code! Edit: Also, thanks to my brother for help with some of the scientific details.

I won't have a list name for this until probably 10+ hours from now, assuming this goes well :)

Edit edit: Several major iterations back, this was originally inspired by a RadioLab episode. I can't recommend them highly enough.
